key_points: Electric current is the flow of electric charges through a conductor
A complete electric circuit is required for current to flow
Electric current can produce heat when it passes through a conductor
Electric current can also create a magnetic field around the conductor
Electromagnets are temporary magnets produced using electric current
The strength of an electromagnet depends on the number of coil turns and the current flowing through it
examples: Electric iron and heater work using the heating effect of electric current
A fuse wire melts when excessive current flows in a circuit
A compass needle deflects when placed near a current-carrying wire
Electric bells use electromagnets to produce sound
Electric cranes use electromagnets to lift heavy iron objects
formulas: Electric circuit = cell/battery + conducting wires + electrical device + switch
Heating effect: electric current passing through a conductor produces heat
Magnetic effect: current flowing through a conductor produces a magnetic field around it
exam_tips: Understand the difference between open circuit and closed circuit
Remember examples of devices using heating effect of electricity
Know how electromagnets work and where they are used
Practice drawing simple electric circuit diagrams with symbols
